meta.title: Nanao Korosa Ski Area
meta.description: The only ski resort on the Noto Peninsula, perfect for a family snow debut paired with exquisite local seafood and Wakura Onsen.

hero.title:
Nanao Korosa
Ski Area
hero.tagline: The only ski resort on the Noto Peninsula, perfect for a family snow debut paired with exquisite local seafood and Wakura Onsen.

expert.title:
A family snow
debut.
expert.lead: The Noto Peninsula's only full ski hill. Watch the sled area from the glass-walled center, climb easily on a 102 m moving walkway, and practice on compact 430 m runs where kids never get lost.
expert.imgAlt: Panorama over Lake Tane—Noto Peninsula's only family-friendly ski area

primary.title: Noto's only ski resort
primary.lead: Ishikawa's largest municipal hill beside Lake Tane in Korosa Lakeside Park. Parents can watch sled play from warm indoor spaces with full slope views from the parking area.
primary.imgAlt: Panorama over Lake Tane—the Noto Peninsula's only ski area

secondary.title: First Snow Mountain Pack
secondary.lead: Move beyond a ¥2,000 lift-only ticket. Bundle moving-walkway access, sled rental, and Wakura Onsen bath tickets—triple spend per guest with snow play and hot springs.

onsen.title:
After skiing,
Wakura Onsen.
onsen.lead: About 30 minutes by car from Korosa. Wakura Onsen Sōyū offers day baths from ¥500—one of the Hokuriku region's finest ways to warm up after a cold day on the slopes.
onsen.imgAlt: Wakura Onsen atmosphere—post-ski bathing and Hokuriku winter scenery

seafood.title:
Slope food,
reimagined as sushi.
seafood.lead: Matsuno Sushi nigiri from ¥1,600, Hachiban Ramen with vegetables—¥500 curry at the ticket machine is beloved, but Nanao Bay seafood downtown raises the whole trip.
seafood.noticeBody: On-site ski rental ¥3,000/day. Web guides for Komatsu Airport rental loops: Nanao city → Korosa → Wakura Onsen.
seafood.imgAlt: Noto-mae sushi from Nanao Bay—premium seafood at Matsuno Sushi and more

tour.title:
Noto winter
premium tour
tour.lead: From ¥2,000 point spending to line-and-area consumption—half- to full-day model courses combining snow play, Nanao dining, and Wakura Onsen.

am.title: Nanao Korosa
am.body: Sled on the moving walkway. Snow debut

noon.title: Nanao gourmet
noon.body: Noto-mae sushi lunch at Matsuno Sushi

drift.title: Moving walkway × sled guide
drift.body: A 102 m domed snow escalator lets toddlers and lift-anxious families climb with ease. During the transition until the pair lift fully reopens in December 2025, promote Korosa as Japan's safest sled-focused snow park.

passGuide.title: First Snow Mountain Pack guide
passGuide.body: Sell integrated digital tickets via Asoview! and similar platforms: moving walkway, sled rental, Wakura Onsen bath, and Nanao dining coupons. Redirect guests to nearby attractions on low-snow days.

snowPlay.title: When you don't ski
snowPlay.body: Turn low-snow risk into opportunity. Sledding, warm indoor breaks at Yamabiko-so—monetize non-ski content without lifts. A symbol of Noto earthquake recovery supporting winter in the region.

access.note: About 20–30 minutes by car from Nanao city · about 30 minutes to Wakura Onsen
access.places: 石川県Ishikawa七尾市Nanao多根町ハカノ谷内555-7555-7 Hakanotani, Tane-machi
access.rentacarLink: Book a rental car at Komatsu Airport
access.rentacarHint: For Nanao and Noto Peninsula loops
